title: Method for producing a coffee substitute
abstract: METHOD FOR PRODUCING A COFFEE SUBSTITUTE In an aspect of the invention, there is provided a method for producing a coffee substitute, the method comprising: (a). carrying out a first fermentation by fermenting a first sulphur-containing food substrate with a first microorganism to form a first fermented product; (b). carrying out a second fermentation by fermenting a second carbohydrate-containing food substrate with a second microorganism to form a second fermented product; (c). mixing the first and second fermented products to form a fermented mixture; and (d). drying the fermented mixture to obtain the coffee substitute.
claims: Claims 1 . A method for producing a coffee substitute, the method comprising: (a). carrying out a first fermentation by fermenting a first sulphur-containing food substrate with a first microorganism to form a first fermented product; (b). carrying out a second fermentation by fermenting a second carbohydrate- containing food substrate with a second microorganism to form a second fermented product; (c). mixing the first and second fermented products to form a fermented mixture; and (d). drying the fermented mixture to obtain the coffee substitute. 2. The method according to claim 1 , further comprising pasteurising the first sulphur- containing food substrate and the second carbohydrate-containing food substrate prior to the fermentation steps (a) and (b). 3. The method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the microorganism is selected from the group consisting of Bacillus subtilis, Aspergillus oryzae and Rhizopus oligosporus. 4. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the sulphur- containing food substrate is soy pulp and the carbohydrate-containing food substrate is selected from the group consisting of spent grain, bread, pineapple pulp and sugarcane molasses. 5. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first microorganism is Bacillus subtilis, and the second microorganism is Aspergillus oryzae and the second carbohydrate-containing food substrate is a mixture comprising bread and spent grain. 6. The method according to claim 5, wherein the first fermentation is carried out at a temperature of 40Â°C for 18 hours, and the second fermentation is carried out at a temperature of 60Â°C for 6 hours. 7. The method according to any one of claims 5 or 6, wherein the soy pulp is inoculated with 2wt% of Bacillus subtilis. 8. The method according to any one of claims 5 or 6, wherein the second carbohydrate- containing food substrate is inoculated with 2wt% of Aspergillus oryzae. 9. The method according to claim 5, wherein the second carbohydrate-containing food substrate is a mixture comprising equal parts of bread and spent grain. 10. The method according to claim 9, wherein the amounts of soy pulp, bread and spent grain fermented are 50 wt%, 25 wt% and 25 wt% respectively. 1 1. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein pasteurising the sulphur-containing food substrate and carbohydrate-containing food substrate is carried out at a temperature of 120Â°C for 40 minutes. 12. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein drying the fermented mixture in step (d) forms a cake, and the drying is carried out at a temperature between 100Â°C to 180Â°C for 3 hours, or the drying is carried out until the water content in the cake is less than 4 wt%. 13. The method according to claim 12, further comprising extruding the cake to form pellets. 14. The method according to claim 13, further comprising roasting the pellets to obtain the coffee substitute. 15. The method according to claim 14, wherein roasting the pellets is carried out at a temperature between 200Â°C to 240Â°C for 20 minutes. 16. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the fermented mixture is formed by mixing the first and second fermented products in equal portions. 17. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein cysteine or methionine is added to the fermentation steps (a) and/or (b), or to the roasting step when dependent on claim 14. 18. The method according to claim 15, wherein the method further comprises grinding the roasted pellets to form a ground coffee substitute product. 19. A method for producing a beverage, the method comprising: (a). making a coffee substitute according to the method according to claim 17; and (b). brewing the coffee substitute to form the beverage. 20. A coffee substitute obtained or obtainable by the process according to any one of claims 1 to 18.
